# Code of Conduct

## Our standard

We want this project to be a useful, respectful place for people with different backgrounds, identities, experience levels, and viewpoints. Be constructive, assume good intent while still addressing impact, and keep criticism focused on the work and its evidence.

Acceptable participation includes:

- explaining decisions and uncertainty clearly,
- welcoming questions without gatekeeping,
- giving actionable technical feedback,
- respecting privacy, consent, and data ownership,
- correcting mistakes and unsupported claims promptly.

Unacceptable behavior includes harassment, discrimination, threats, sexualized attention, doxxing, publishing private data, sustained personal attacks, or disrupting project spaces.

## Scope and enforcement

This policy applies in repository discussions, reviews, project events, and public representation of the project. Maintainers may edit or remove contributions, restrict participation, or take other proportionate action when behavior violates this policy.

Report conduct concerns privately to the maintainer through the contact methods on the [maintainer's GitHub profile](https://github.com/Borda). Do not include more personal data than necessary. Reports will be handled as privately as practical, but no fixed response-time guarantee is made.

Good-faith reports and appeals will be considered without retaliation. A maintainer directly involved in a concern should recurse themselves when another suitable reviewer is available.
